speaker:
FIRST
               
              GAMEKEEPER
text: Under this thick-grown brake we’ll shroud ourselves,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GAMEKEEPER
text: For through this laund anon the deer will come;

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GAMEKEEPER
text: And in this covert will we make our stand,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GAMEKEEPER
text: Culling the principal of all the deer.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GAMEKEEPER
text: I’ll stay above the hill, so both may shoot.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GAMEKEEPER
text: That cannot be. The noise of thy crossbow

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GAMEKEEPER
text: Will scare the herd, and so my shoot is lost.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GAMEKEEPER
text: Here stand we both, and aim we at the best.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GAMEKEEPER
text: And for the time shall not seem tedious,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GAMEKEEPER
text: I’ll tell thee what befell me on a day

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GAMEKEEPER
text: In this self place where now we mean to stand.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GAMEKEEPER
text: Here comes a man; let’s stay till he be past.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: From Scotland am I stol’n, even of pure love,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: To greet mine own land with my wishful sight.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: No, Harry, Harry, ’tis no land of thine!

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Thy place is filled, thy scepter wrung from thee,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Thy balm washed off wherewith thou wast anointed.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: No bending knee will call thee Caesar now,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: No humble suitors press to speak for right,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: No, not a man comes for redress of thee;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: For how can I help them an not myself?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GAMEKEEPER
text: Ay, here’s a deer whose skin’s a keeper’s fee.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GAMEKEEPER
text: This is the quondam king. Let’s seize upon him.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Let me embrace the sour adversaries,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: For wise men say it is the wisest course.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GAMEKEEPER
text: Why linger we? Let us lay hands upon him.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GAMEKEEPER
text: Forbear awhile; we’ll hear a little more.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: My queen and son are gone to France for aid,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And, as I hear, the great commanding Warwick

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Is thither gone to crave the French king’s sister

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: To wife for Edward. If this news be true,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Poor queen and son, your labor is but lost,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: For Warwick is a subtle orator,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And Lewis a prince soon won with moving words.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: By this account, then, Margaret may win him,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: For she’s a woman to be pitied much.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Her sighs will make a batt’ry in his breast,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Her tears will pierce into a marble heart.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: The tiger will be mild whiles she doth mourn,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And Nero will be tainted with remorse

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: To hear and see her plaints, her brinish tears.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Ay, but she’s come to beg, Warwick to give;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: She on his left side craving aid for Henry;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: He on his right asking a wife for Edward.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: She weeps and says her Henry is deposed;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: He smiles and says his Edward is installed;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: That she, poor wretch, for grief can speak no more,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Whiles Warwick tells his title, smooths the wrong,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Inferreth arguments of mighty strength,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And in conclusion wins the King from her

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: With promise of his sister and what else

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: To strengthen and support King Edward’s place.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: O Margaret, thus ’twill be, and thou, poor soul,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Art then forsaken, as thou went’st forlorn.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GAMEKEEPER
text: Say, what art thou that talk’st of kings and queens?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: More than I seem, and less than I was born to:

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: A man at least, for less I should not be;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And men may talk of kings, and why not I?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GAMEKEEPER
text: Ay, but thou talk’st as if thou wert a king.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Why, so I am in mind, and that’s enough.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GAMEKEEPER
text: But if thou be a king, where is thy crown?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: My crown is in my heart, not on my head;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Not decked with diamonds and Indian stones,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Nor to be seen. My crown is called content;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: A crown it is that seldom kings enjoy.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GAMEKEEPER
text: Well, if you be a king crowned with content,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GAMEKEEPER
text: Your crown content and you must be contented

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GAMEKEEPER
text: To go along with us. For, as we think,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GAMEKEEPER
text: You are the king King Edward hath deposed;

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GAMEKEEPER
text: And we his subjects sworn in all allegiance

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GAMEKEEPER
text: Will apprehend you as his enemy.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: But did you never swear and break an oath?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GAMEKEEPER
text: No, never such an oath, nor will not now.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Where did you dwell when I was King of England?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GAMEKEEPER
text: Here in this country, where we now remain.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I was anointed king at nine months old.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: My father and my grandfather were kings,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And you were sworn true subjects unto me.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And tell me, then, have you not broke your oaths?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GAMEKEEPER
text: No, for we were subjects but while you were king.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Why, am I dead? Do I not breathe a man?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Ah, simple men, you know not what you swear.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Look as I blow this feather from my face

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And as the air blows it to me again,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Obeying with my wind when I do blow

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And yielding to another when it blows,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Commanded always by the greater gust,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Such is the lightness of you common men.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: But do not break your oaths, for of that sin

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: My mild entreaty shall not make you guilty.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Go where you will, the King shall be commanded,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And be you kings: command, and I’ll obey.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GAMEKEEPER
text: We are true subjects to the King, King Edward.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: So would you be again to Henry

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: If he were seated as King Edward is.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GAMEKEEPER
text: We charge you in God’s name and the King’s

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GAMEKEEPER
text: To go with us unto the officers.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: In God’s name, lead. Your king’s name be obeyed,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And what God will, that let your king perform.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And what he will, I humbly yield unto.
